sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 20.91 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.20)

statement.latex: A group$G$is said to be stable if for any first-order formula$( , )$(in the first-order language of groups$\{\, \,, {}^{-1}, 1\}$) there exists a natural number$n$such that whenever there exist sequences of tuples of$G$,$(a_i)_{i<m}$,$(b_i)_{i<m}$with$G (a_i, b_j)$if and only if$i<j$, then$m n$. Sela proved that all torsion-free hyperbolic groups are stable. [r]{a)} Is there a non-stable hyperboli...
